operating_state: Total net sales increased 36% to $81.4 billion in Q3 2021, driven by growth across all product and services categories. iPhone revenue grew 50% from new models launched in Q1 2021, while Mac, iPad, and Wearables posted double-digit growth from product refreshes and strong demand. Services revenue expanded 33% primarily from advertising, App Store, and AppleCare growth. All geographic segments posted growth with Greater China leading and favorable foreign exchange impacts across international markets.
cost_structure: Total gross margin expanded to 43.3% from 38.0% in the prior year quarter, with products gross margin improving to 36.0% due to different product mix, foreign currency strength, and improved leverage. Services gross margin increased to 69.8% driven by different services mix and improved leverage, partially offset by higher services costs. Operating expenses declined as a percentage of sales to 14% from 16% despite absolute increases from headcount and material costs.
material_events: The company launched iMac and iPad Pro powered by Apple M1 chip, Apple TV 4K, AirTag accessory, and Apple Podcasts Subscriptions during the quarter. Apple announced major operating system updates for iOS, macOS, iPadOS, and watchOS expected in fall 2021. The company repurchased $22.5 billion of common stock and paid dividends during Q3 2021.
macro_exposures: COVID-19 pandemic continued affecting business operations with retail store and channel partner closures, though substantially all locations have reopened subject to operating restrictions. The company benefited from favorable foreign currency movements across multiple international regions. Supply chain operations remained impacted by pandemic-related disruptions affecting manufacturing partners and component suppliers.
forward_outlook: Management maintains plans for annual dividend increases subject to board approval with quarterly dividend at $0.22 per share. The company maintains share repurchase authorization with significant capacity remaining. Apple continues focused R&D investments deemed critical for future growth and competitive positioning in new product and service development.
uncertainty_profile: The extent of ongoing COVID-19 impact remains uncertain depending on pandemic duration and global economic recovery affecting consumer demand. The company maintains significant European escrow obligations related to pending State Aid Decision legal proceedings and manufacturing purchase obligations under primarily noncancelable supplier arrangements.
